content.text:
{"success":true,"process_rule_id":"J09A","process_rule":"1. Create working table.
   - From `decision_table`, select all rows where `TOURS` starts with the prefix `"J09A"` (string prefix match).
   - Store the result as a new table named `J09A_temp`.

2. Add the decision column.
   - Add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` (text) to `J09A_temp`.
   - Initialize `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to NULL (empty) for all rows.

3. Set base ENTSCHEIDUNG by number of meters per house.
   - For each distinct value of `HAUS` in `J09A_temp`:
     - Compute `eq_count` = count of distinct non-NULL `EQUNR` values in `J09A_temp` for this `HAUS`.
     - If `eq_count < 3`:
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "KSA"` for all rows in `J09A_temp` with this `HAUS`.
     - Else:
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "EVU"` for all rows in `J09A_temp` with this `HAUS`.

4. Override ENTSCHEIDUNG based on house number and address area.
   - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
     - Let `hn = HOUSE_NUM1` for this row.
     - Let `area = CITY2` for this row (assume `CITY2` stores the address area, e.g. “Wiblingwerde”, “Breckerfeld”).
     - If (`hn = 0`) OR (`500 <= hn <= 599`) OR (`900 <= hn <= 999`) OR (`area` is `"Wiblingwerde"` or `"Breckerfeld"`):
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "EVU"` for this row (this overrides any previous value such as `"KSA"`).
     - Else:
       - Leave `ENTSCHEIDUNG` unchanged for this row.

5. Join with EABLG (installation / meter reading header).
   - Replace `J09A_temp` with the result of a LEFT JOIN of `J09A_temp` to `EABLG`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.ANLAGE` (or `INSTALLATION`; use the installation ID field from `J09A_temp`) = `EABLG.INSTALLATION` (or equivalent field).
   - Ensure all rows from `J09A_temp` are preserved even if no matching row exists in `EABLG`.

6. Join with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER by EQUNR.
   - LEFT JOIN `NET_MASTER` to the current `J09A_temp` on `EQUNR`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.EQUNR = NET_MASTER.EQUNR`.
   - LEFT JOIN `SALES_MASTER` to the current `J09A_temp` on `EQUNR`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.EQUNR = SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`.
   - After these joins, `J09A_temp` should contain columns from `decision_table`/`EABLG` plus NET and SALES data.

7. Prefer SALES data over NET data for each row.
   - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
     - Determine whether SALES data is present in this row (e.g. `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR` or another key SALES column is not NULL).
     - If SALES data is present:
       - For any business fields that exist in both `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` (e.g. `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`, etc.):
         - Take the value from the SALES columns as the final value for this row.
       - Set NET-specific columns for this row to NULL/empty (so that only SALES data is effectively used).
     - Else:
       - Keep the NET_MASTER values for these fields as the final values.
       - Set SALES-specific columns to NULL/empty (so that only NET data is effectively used).

8. Determine EQUNR that require MANDATORY_READING based on EABL.
   - Build a set `mandatory_eq` using table `EABL`:
     - For each distinct `EQUNR` present in `EABL`:
       - Let `has_2023` = TRUE if there exists at least one `EABL` row for this `EQUNR` where:
         - `ISTABLART_TXT = "Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP"`, and
         - the record creation year is 2023 (e.g. `YEAR(record_created_on) = 2023`).
         Otherwise set `has_2023 = FALSE`.
       - Let `has_2024` = TRUE if there exists at least one `EABL` row for this `EQUNR` where:
         - `ISTABLART_TXT = "Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP"`, and
         - the record creation year is 2024 (e.g. `YEAR(record_created_on) = 2024`).
         Otherwise set `has_2024 = FALSE`.
       - If (`has_2023 = TRUE` AND `has_2024 = TRUE`):
         - Add this `EQUNR` to the set `mandatory_eq`.
       - Else:
         - Do not add this `EQUNR` to `mandatory_eq`.
   - Update `J09A_temp` using `mandatory_eq`:
     - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
       - If `EQUNR` of this row is in `mandatory_eq`:
         - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "MANDATORY_READING"` for this row (this overrides `"KSA"` or `"EVU"`).
       - Else:
         - Leave `ENTSCHEIDUNG` unchanged.

9. Export MANDATORY_READING entries to CSV `J09A_MANDATORY_READING`.
   - From `J09A_temp`, select all rows where `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "MANDATORY_READING"`.
   - In the export, include exactly the following columns, in this order:
     - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`  (map from the appropriate EABLG/EABL document number column)
     - `HAUS`
     - `ANLAGE`
     - `ME_MA_ID`
     - `EQUNR`
     - `HOUSE_NUM1`
     - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
     - `TITLE`
     - `FORENAME`
     - `SURNAME`
     - `STREET`
     - `STREETNO`
     - `POST_CODE1`
     - `CITY1`
     - `CITY2`
     - `EMAIL`
   - Write these rows to a CSV file named `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv`.

10. Send MANDATORY_READING CSV to internal team.
    - Compose an email to `mandatory.reading@provider.com`.
    - Attach the file `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv`.
    - Send the email.

11. Export non-MANDATORY_READING entries to CSV `J09A`.
    - From `J09A_temp`, select all rows where `ENTSCHEIDUNG` is NOT `"MANDATORY_READING"` (i.e. typically `"EVU"` or `"KSA"`).
    - In the export, include exactly the following columns, in this order:
      - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
      - `HAUS`
      - `ANLAGE`
      - `ME_MA_ID`
      - `EQUNR`
      - `HOUSE_NUM1`
      - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
      - `TITLE`
      - `FORENAME`
      - `SURNAME`
      - `STREET`
      - `STREETNO`
      - `POST_CODE1`
      - `CITY1`
      - `CITY2`
      - `EMAIL`
    - Write these rows to a CSV file named `J09A.csv`.

12. Send EVU CSV to EVU team.
    - Compose an email to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
    - Attach the file `J09A.csv`.
    - Send the email.

13. Clean up temporary data.
    - Delete (drop) the table `J09A_temp` after confirming that both CSV exports (`J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv` and `J09A.csv`) have been successfully created and sent."}

structured_content.process_rule:
1. Create working table.
   - From `decision_table`, select all rows where `TOURS` starts with the prefix `"J09A"` (string prefix match).
   - Store the result as a new table named `J09A_temp`.

2. Add the decision column.
   - Add a new column `ENTSCHEIDUNG` (text) to `J09A_temp`.
   - Initialize `ENTSCHEIDUNG` to NULL (empty) for all rows.

3. Set base ENTSCHEIDUNG by number of meters per house.
   - For each distinct value of `HAUS` in `J09A_temp`:
     - Compute `eq_count` = count of distinct non-NULL `EQUNR` values in `J09A_temp` for this `HAUS`.
     - If `eq_count < 3`:
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "KSA"` for all rows in `J09A_temp` with this `HAUS`.
     - Else:
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "EVU"` for all rows in `J09A_temp` with this `HAUS`.

4. Override ENTSCHEIDUNG based on house number and address area.
   - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
     - Let `hn = HOUSE_NUM1` for this row.
     - Let `area = CITY2` for this row (assume `CITY2` stores the address area, e.g. “Wiblingwerde”, “Breckerfeld”).
     - If (`hn = 0`) OR (`500 <= hn <= 599`) OR (`900 <= hn <= 999`) OR (`area` is `"Wiblingwerde"` or `"Breckerfeld"`):
       - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "EVU"` for this row (this overrides any previous value such as `"KSA"`).
     - Else:
       - Leave `ENTSCHEIDUNG` unchanged for this row.

5. Join with EABLG (installation / meter reading header).
   - Replace `J09A_temp` with the result of a LEFT JOIN of `J09A_temp` to `EABLG`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.ANLAGE` (or `INSTALLATION`; use the installation ID field from `J09A_temp`) = `EABLG.INSTALLATION` (or equivalent field).
   - Ensure all rows from `J09A_temp` are preserved even if no matching row exists in `EABLG`.

6. Join with NET_MASTER and SALES_MASTER by EQUNR.
   - LEFT JOIN `NET_MASTER` to the current `J09A_temp` on `EQUNR`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.EQUNR = NET_MASTER.EQUNR`.
   - LEFT JOIN `SALES_MASTER` to the current `J09A_temp` on `EQUNR`:
     - Join condition: `J09A_temp.EQUNR = SALES_MASTER.EQUNR`.
   - After these joins, `J09A_temp` should contain columns from `decision_table`/`EABLG` plus NET and SALES data.

7. Prefer SALES data over NET data for each row.
   - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
     - Determine whether SALES data is present in this row (e.g. `SALES_MASTER.EQUNR` or another key SALES column is not NULL).
     - If SALES data is present:
       - For any business fields that exist in both `NET_MASTER` and `SALES_MASTER` (e.g. `TITLE`, `FORENAME`, `SURNAME`, `STREET`, `STREETNO`, `POST_CODE1`, `CITY1`, `CITY2`, `EMAIL`, etc.):
         - Take the value from the SALES columns as the final value for this row.
       - Set NET-specific columns for this row to NULL/empty (so that only SALES data is effectively used).
     - Else:
       - Keep the NET_MASTER values for these fields as the final values.
       - Set SALES-specific columns to NULL/empty (so that only NET data is effectively used).

8. Determine EQUNR that require MANDATORY_READING based on EABL.
   - Build a set `mandatory_eq` using table `EABL`:
     - For each distinct `EQUNR` present in `EABL`:
       - Let `has_2023` = TRUE if there exists at least one `EABL` row for this `EQUNR` where:
         - `ISTABLART_TXT = "Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP"`, and
         - the record creation year is 2023 (e.g. `YEAR(record_created_on) = 2023`).
         Otherwise set `has_2023 = FALSE`.
       - Let `has_2024` = TRUE if there exists at least one `EABL` row for this `EQUNR` where:
         - `ISTABLART_TXT = "Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP"`, and
         - the record creation year is 2024 (e.g. `YEAR(record_created_on) = 2024`).
         Otherwise set `has_2024 = FALSE`.
       - If (`has_2023 = TRUE` AND `has_2024 = TRUE`):
         - Add this `EQUNR` to the set `mandatory_eq`.
       - Else:
         - Do not add this `EQUNR` to `mandatory_eq`.
   - Update `J09A_temp` using `mandatory_eq`:
     - For each row in `J09A_temp`:
       - If `EQUNR` of this row is in `mandatory_eq`:
         - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "MANDATORY_READING"` for this row (this overrides `"KSA"` or `"EVU"`).
       - Else:
         - Leave `ENTSCHEIDUNG` unchanged.

9. Export MANDATORY_READING entries to CSV `J09A_MANDATORY_READING`.
   - From `J09A_temp`, select all rows where `ENTSCHEIDUNG = "MANDATORY_READING"`.
   - In the export, include exactly the following columns, in this order:
     - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`  (map from the appropriate EABLG/EABL document number column)
     - `HAUS`
     - `ANLAGE`
     - `ME_MA_ID`
     - `EQUNR`
     - `HOUSE_NUM1`
     - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
     - `TITLE`
     - `FORENAME`
     - `SURNAME`
     - `STREET`
     - `STREETNO`
     - `POST_CODE1`
     - `CITY1`
     - `CITY2`
     - `EMAIL`
   - Write these rows to a CSV file named `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv`.

10. Send MANDATORY_READING CSV to internal team.
    - Compose an email to `mandatory.reading@provider.com`.
    - Attach the file `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv`.
    - Send the email.

11. Export non-MANDATORY_READING entries to CSV `J09A`.
    - From `J09A_temp`, select all rows where `ENTSCHEIDUNG` is NOT `"MANDATORY_READING"` (i.e. typically `"EVU"` or `"KSA"`).
    - In the export, include exactly the following columns, in this order:
      - `Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.`
      - `HAUS`
      - `ANLAGE`
      - `ME_MA_ID`
      - `EQUNR`
      - `HOUSE_NUM1`
      - `ENTSCHEIDUNG`
      - `TITLE`
      - `FORENAME`
      - `SURNAME`
      - `STREET`
      - `STREETNO`
      - `POST_CODE1`
      - `CITY1`
      - `CITY2`
      - `EMAIL`
    - Write these rows to a CSV file named `J09A.csv`.

12. Send EVU CSV to EVU team.
    - Compose an email to `meter.readings@evu.com`.
    - Attach the file `J09A.csv`.
    - Send the email.

13. Clean up temporary data.
    - Delete (drop) the table `J09A_temp` after confirming that both CSV exports (`J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv` and `J09A.csv`) have been successfully created and sent.

{"success":true,"schema":"# Table: net_master
## column_name (type) description
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)
FORENAME (TEXT) First name
SURNAME (TEXT) Last name/surname
STREET (TEXT) Street name
STREETNO (TEXT) Street number
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EMAIL (TEXT) Email address

# Table: sales_master
## column_name (type) description
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)
FORENAME (TEXT) First name
SURNAME (TEXT) Last name/surname
STREET (TEXT) Street name
STREETNO (TEXT) Street number
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EMAIL (TEXT) Email address

# Table: decision_table
## column_name (type) description
HAUS (TEXT) House identifier
ANLAGE (TEXT) Installation/facility identifier
ABLEINH (TEXT) Reading unit identifier
TOUR (TEXT) Tour/route identifier
ME_MA_ID (TEXT) Meter/Measuring point ID
CITY1 (TEXT) City name
CITY2 (TEXT) District/additional city information
HOUSE_NUM1 (INTEGER) House/building number
POST_CODE1 (INTEGER) Postal code
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number

# Table: eabl
## column_name (type) description
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No. (TEXT) Meter Reading Document Number
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
Geplante Ableseart (INTEGER) Planned reading type
MR type (TEXT) Meter reading type code
MR TYPE TEXT (TEXT) Meter Reading type description
MR category (TEXT) Meter reading category
ISTABLART_TXT (TEXT) Actual reading type description (SAP)
LOGIKZW (TEXT) Logic indicator
KENNZIFF (TEXT) Identification number
Record created on (TEXT) Record creation date
Object changed on (TEXT) Object modification date

# Table: eablg
## column_name (type) description
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No. (TEXT) Meter Reading Document Number
Installation (TEXT) Installation identifier
Meter Reading reason (INTEGER) Meter reading reason code
MR Reason - Text (TEXT) Meter reading reason description
Scheduled MR Date (TEXT) Scheduled meter reading date
Meter Reading unit (TEXT) Meter reading unit
Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No..1 (INTEGER) Secondary/duplicate Meter Reading Document Number

# Table: eanl
## column_name (type) description
Installation (TEXT) Installation identifier
Installation type (TEXT) Type of installation
Record created on (TEXT) Record creation date
Object changed on (TEXT) Object modification date
SPARTE_TEXT (TEXT) Division/sector description
AKLASSE (TEXT) Customer class code
AKLASSE_TEXT (TEXT) Customer class description
TARIFTYP (TEXT) Tariff type code
TARIFTYP_TEXT (TEXT) Tariff type description
BRANCHE (TEXT) Industry/sector code
BRANCHE_TEXT (TEXT) Industry/sector description
ABLEINH (TEXT) Reading unit identifier
ME_MA_ID (TEXT) Meter/Measuring point ID
HAUS (TEXT) House identifier

# Table: process_rules
## column_name (type) description
process_rule_id (TEXT) PRIMARY KEY - Process rule identifier
process_rule (TEXT) NOT NULL - Process rule text/description"}

## column_name (type) description
EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number
TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)

SURNAME (TEXT) Last name/surname

# Table: sales_master

TITLE (TEXT) Title (Mr., Mrs., Dr., etc.)

STREET (TEXT) Street name

# Table: decision_table

HAUS (TEXT) House identifier

EQUNR (TEXT) Equipment Number

# Table: eabl

Geplante Ableseart (INTEGER) Planned reading type

# Table: eablg

# Table: process_rules
## column_name (type) description
process_rule_id (TEXT) PRIMARY KEY - Process rule identifier
process_rule (TEXT) NOT NULL - Process rule text/description

args.sqlite_command: DROP TABLE IF EXISTS J09A_temp;

CREATE TABLE J09A_temp AS
  SELECT dt.*
  FROM decision_table dt
  WHERE dt.TOUR LIKE 'J09A%';
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN ENTSCHEIDUNG TEXT;

UPDATE J09A_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = CASE
  WHEN eq_count < 3 THEN 'KSA'
  ELSE 'EVU'
END
FROM (
  SELECT HAUS, COUNT(DISTINCT EQUNR) AS eq_count
  FROM J09A_temp
  WHERE EQUNR IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY HAUS
) AS counts
WHERE J09A_temp.HAUS = counts.HAUS;

UPDATE J09A_temp
SET ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'EVU'
WHERE HOUSE_NUM1 = 0
   OR (HOUSE_NUM1 BETWEEN 500 AND 599)
   OR (HOUSE_NUM1 BETWEEN 900 AND 999)
   OR CITY2 IN ('Wiblingwerde', 'Breckerfeld');

CREATE TABLE J09A_temp_joined AS
SELECT t.*, g."Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No." AS MR_Doc_No
FROM J09A_temp t
LEFT JOIN eablg g
  ON t.ANLAGE = g.Installation;
DROP TABLE J09A_temp;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp_joined RENAME TO J09A_temp;

CREATE TABLE J09A_temp_joined2 AS
SELECT t.*, 
       n.TITLE AS NET_TITLE, n.FORENAME AS NET_FORENAME, n.SURNAME AS NET_SURNAME,
       n.STREET AS NET_STREET, n.STREETNO AS NET_STREETNO,
       n.POST_CODE1 AS NET_POST_CODE1, n.CITY1 AS NET_CITY1, n.CITY2 AS NET_CITY2,
       n.EMAIL AS NET_EMAIL,
       s.TITLE AS SALES_TITLE, s.FORENAME AS SALES_FORENAME, s.SURNAME AS SALES_SURNAME,
       s.STREET AS SALES_STREET, s.STREETNO AS SALES_STREETNO,
       s.POST_CODE1 AS SALES_POST_CODE1, s.CITY1 AS SALES_CITY1, s.CITY2 AS SALES_CITY2,
       s.EMAIL AS SALES_EMAIL
FROM J09A_temp t
LEFT JOIN net_master n ON t.EQUNR = n.EQUNR
LEFT JOIN sales_master s ON t.EQUNR = s.EQUNR;
DROP TABLE J09A_temp;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp_joined2 RENAME TO J09A_temp;

ALTER TABLE J09A_temp
ADD COLUMN TITLE TEXT,
ADD COLUMN FORENAME TEXT,
ADD COLUMN SURNAME TEXT,
ADD COLUMN STREET TEXT,
ADD COLUMN STREETNO TEXT,
ADD COLUMN POST_CODE1 INTEGER,
ADD COLUMN CITY1 TEXT,
ADD COLUMN CITY2 TEXT,
ADD COLUMN EMAIL TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN TITLE TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN FORENAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN SURNAME TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN STREET TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN STREETNO TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN POST_CODE1 INTEGER;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN CITY1 TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN CITY2 TEXT;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp ADD COLUMN EMAIL TEXT;

UPDATE J09A_temp
SET 
  TITLE = CASE WHEN SALES_TITLE IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_TITLE ELSE NET_TITLE END,
  FORENAME = CASE WHEN SALES_FORENAME IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_FORENAME ELSE NET_FORENAME END,
  SURNAME = CASE WHEN SALES_SURNAME IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_SURNAME ELSE NET_SURNAME END,
  STREET = CASE WHEN SALES_STREET IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_STREET ELSE NET_STREET END,
  STREETNO = CASE WHEN SALES_STREETNO IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_STREETNO ELSE NET_STREETNO END,
  POST_CODE1 = CASE WHEN SALES_POST_CODE1 IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_POST_CODE1 ELSE NET_POST_CODE1 END,
  CITY1 = CASE WHEN SALES_CITY1 IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_CITY1 ELSE NET_CITY1 END,
  CITY2 = CASE WHEN SALES_CITY2 IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_CITY2 ELSE NET_CITY2 END,
  EMAIL = CASE WHEN SALES_EMAIL IS NOT NULL THEN SALES_EMAIL ELSE NET_EMAIL END;

ALTER TABLE J09A_temp
DROP COLUMN NET_TITLE;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_FORENAME;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_SURNAME;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_STREET;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_STREETNO;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_POST_CODE1;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_CITY1;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_CITY2;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN NET_EMAIL;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_TITLE;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_FORENAME;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_SURNAME;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_STREET;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_STREETNO;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_POST_CODE1;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_CITY1;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_CITY2;
ALTER TABLE J09A_temp DROP COLUMN SALES_EMAIL;

CREATE TABLE mandatory_eq AS
SELECT EQUNR
FROM (
  SELECT EQUNR,
         SUM(CASE WHEN ISTABLART_TXT = 'Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP' AND substr("Record created on", 1, 4) = '2023' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_2023,
         SUM(CASE WHEN ISTABLART_TXT = 'Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP' AND substr("Record created on", 1, 4) = '2024' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_2024
  FROM eabl
  GROUP BY EQUNR
) AS x
WHERE has_2023 > 0 AND has_2024 > 0;

args.sql_query:
SELECT
  MR_Doc_No AS "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  HAUS,
  ANLAGE,
  ME_MA_ID,
  EQUNR,
  HOUSE_NUM1,
  ENTSCHEIDUNG,
  TITLE,
  FORENAME,
  SURNAME,
  STREET,
  STREETNO,
  POST_CODE1,
  CITY1,
  CITY2,
  EMAIL
FROM J09A_temp
WHERE ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'MANDATORY_READING';

SELECT
  MR_Doc_No AS "Meter Reading (MR) Doc. No.",
  HAUS,
  ANLAGE,
  ME_MA_ID,
  EQUNR,
  HOUSE_NUM1,
  ENTSCHEIDUNG,
  TITLE,
  FORENAME,
  SURNAME,
  STREET,
  STREETNO,
  POST_CODE1,
  CITY1,
  CITY2,
  EMAIL
FROM J09A_temp
WHERE ENTSCHEIDUNG <> 'MANDATORY_READING';

kwargs.content:
The process rule `J09A` has been executed end-to-end:

- Created and populated the working table `J09A_temp` from `decision_table` for all tours starting with `J09A`.
- Added and populated `ENTSCHEIDUNG` based on:
  - Number of distinct meters (`EQUNR`) per house (`HAUS`) → `KSA` if < 3, otherwise `EVU`.
  - Overrides for specific house numbers and areas (house number ranges 0, 500–599, 900–999, and areas Wiblingwerde/Breckerfeld → `EVU`).
- Joined in header data from `EABLG` (meter reading document number per installation).
- Joined in NET and SALES master data by `EQUNR` and resolved business fields so that SALES data is preferred where available (NET values are used only when SALES is missing).
- Determined `MANDATORY_READING` meters via `EABL`:
  - Built `mandatory_eq` = all `EQUNR` that have at least one `Maschinelle Schätzung - SAP` record in 2023 and at least one in 2024.
  - Set `ENTSCHEIDUNG = 'MANDATORY_READING'` for those meters.
- Exported two CSVs:
  - `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv` (666 rows) with the specified columns/order.
  - `J09A.csv` (20 200 rows) with the specified columns/order for all non‑`MANDATORY_READING` entries.
- “Sent” (logged metadata for) emails:
  - To `mandatory.reading@provider.com` with `J09A_MANDATORY_READING.csv`.
  - To `meter.readings@evu.com` with `J09A.csv`.
- Cleaned up temporary tables `J09A_temp` and `mandatory_eq`.

If you’d like, I can show or adjust the exact SQL used for any of the steps.
